name: Autocomplete
description: A text input with a filterable suggestion dropdown. Built on Base UI Autocomplete with StyleX styling. Input-only style with built-in clear button.

Root.description: State container. Manages filtering, open state, and keyboard navigation. Renders no DOM element.

items.description: Items or grouped items to display in the suggestion list.

defaultValue.description: Initial input value for uncontrolled mode.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when the input value changes.

onOpenChange.description: Callback fired when the popup opens or closes.

filter.description: Custom filter function. Built-in contains filter used by default.

filteredItems.description: Externally filtered items (for async search). Bypasses built-in filter.

autoHighlight.description: Auto-highlight the first matching item as user types.

limit.description: Maximum number of visible items. -1 for unlimited.

size.description: Size of the input and dropdown items. Aligns with Button sizes (sm=32px, md=36px, lg=40px).

Input.description: Text input with built-in clear button. Clear button appears automatically when input has text.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown when input is empty.

startAddon.description: An icon or element positioned at the start of the input.

dataAttributes.data-popup-open: Present when the suggestion popup is open.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the input is disabled.
dataAttributes.data-readonly: Present when the input is read-only.
dataAttributes.data-required: Present when the input is required.
dataAttributes.data-focused: Present when the input is focused.
dataAttributes.data-filled: Present when the input has a value.

Popup.description: Dropdown containing suggestion items. Portal, Positioner, and List are internalized.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the popup is open.
dataAttributes.data-closed: Present when the popup is closed.
dataAttributes.data-empty: Present when no items match the filter.

Item.description: A single suggestion row in the dropdown.

value.description: The item value. Used for filtering and selection.

dataAttributes.data-highlighted: Present when the item has keyboard/pointer focus.
dataAttributes.data-selected: Present when the item matches the current input value.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

Empty.description: Shown when no items match the current filter query.

Group.description: Groups related items with a label.

GroupLabel.description: Heading for a group of items.

cssRequirements.description: Popup fade and slide animation requires global CSS rules inside @layer priority1.

intents.reasoning: Autocomplete is the primary component for text inputs that show filtered suggestions as the user types.
intents.composition:
<Autocomplete.Root items={items}>
  <Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Search..." />
  <Autocomplete.Popup>
    <Autocomplete.Empty>No results found.</Autocomplete.Empty>
    {(item) => (
      <Autocomplete.Item key={item.id} value={item}>
        {item.value}
      </Autocomplete.Item>
    )}
  </Autocomplete.Popup>
</Autocomplete.Root>

intents.reasoning: Autocomplete provides the text-input-with-filtered-list pattern used in command palettes. Combine with a Dialog for the overlay.
intents.composition:
<Autocomplete.Root items={commands}>
  <Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Type a command..." />
  <Autocomplete.Popup>
    <Autocomplete.Empty>No commands found.</Autocomplete.Empty>
    {(cmd) => (
      <Autocomplete.Item key={cmd.id} value={cmd}>
        {cmd.label}
      </Autocomplete.Item>
    )}
  </Autocomplete.Popup>
</Autocomplete.Root>

intents.reasoning: Autocomplete works well for searching through and selecting from a list of tags or labels.
intents.composition:
<Autocomplete.Root items={tags}>
  <Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Search tags..." />
  <Autocomplete.Popup>
    <Autocomplete.Empty>No tags found.</Autocomplete.Empty>
    {(tag) => (
      <Autocomplete.Item key={tag.id} value={tag}>
        {tag.value}
      </Autocomplete.Item>
    )}
  </Autocomplete.Popup>
</Autocomplete.Root>

avoidWhen.scenario: User must select from a fixed set of options (no free text)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Select or Combobox instead. Autocomplete allows free-form text input — the value is the text itself, not a selection.
Autocomplete does not support multiple selection. Use Combobox with multiple mode which provides Chips parts.
Use Input directly. Autocomplete adds overhead for the popup and filtering logic that is unnecessary without suggestions.

examples.name: basic
grouped
examples.code:
<Autocomplete.Root items={groupedItems}>
  <Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Search produce..." />
  <Autocomplete.Popup>
    <Autocomplete.Empty>No results.</Autocomplete.Empty>
    {(group) => (
      <Autocomplete.Group key={group.label}>
        <Autocomplete.GroupLabel>{group.label}</Autocomplete.GroupLabel>
        {group.items.map((item) => (
          <Autocomplete.Item key={item.id} value={item}>
            {item.value}
          </Autocomplete.Item>
        ))}
      </Autocomplete.Group>
    )}
  </Autocomplete.Popup>
</Autocomplete.Root>

examples.name: auto-highlight
examples.description: First match auto-highlighted as user types
examples.code:
<Autocomplete.Root items={items} autoHighlight>
  <Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Start typing..." />
  <Autocomplete.Popup>
    <Autocomplete.Empty>No results.</Autocomplete.Empty>
    {(item) => (
      <Autocomplete.Item key={item.id} value={item}>
        {item.value}
      </Autocomplete.Item>
    )}
  </Autocomplete.Popup>
</Autocomplete.Root>
